intro: The biggest real split between Feather Icons and Sketch is lock-in posture: Feather Icons carries low lock-in (oss svg icon set), while Sketch carries medium lock-in (mac-only, proprietary but export supported). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. Feather Icons is built for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and is not a fit for new projects needing active updates, more icons, or multiple weights. Sketch is built for solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools and is not a fit for distributed teams, windows/linux users, or orgs standardizing on figma. On the scoring side, Feather Icons rates 4/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Sketch rates 4/5 and 5/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Feather Icons you accept that maintenance has been slow for years, while Sketch comes with the reality that mac-only limits distributed team adoption. Neither side is free.
quick_take: Feather Icons is for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style; Sketch is for solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over.
verdict_a: Choose Feather Icons if you are legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and can accept low lock-in (oss svg icon set).
verdict_b: Choose Sketch if you are solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools and need medium lock-in (mac-only, proprietary but export supported).

faq.q: Is Feather Icons cheaper than Sketch?
faq.a: Feather Icons uses a free model, while Sketch uses a subscription model; Feather Icons has a free tier and Sketch does not. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Feather Icons to Sketch?
faq.a: Feather Icons has low lock-in (oss svg icon set); Sketch has medium lock-in (mac-only, proprietary but export supported). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Feather Icons; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both Feather Icons and Sketch score 4/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is Sketch a good alternative to Feather Icons?
faq.a: Sketch is built for solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools and explicitly not for distributed teams, windows/linux users, or orgs standardizing on figma, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Feather Icons for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
